clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall perform the UI design services described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [PROJECT_NAME], covering [NUMBER_OF_SCREENS] screens.
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.
Where the Services include an interactive prototype, the Service Provider shall deliver it using [PROTOTYPE_TOOL], covering the flows described in [PROTOTYPE_SCOPE].

Where the Client requests developer handoff support, the Service Provider shall provide up to [DEVELOPER_SUPPORT_HOURS] hours of assistance at [DEVELOPER_SUPPORT_RATE] per hour beyond that allotment. The Service Provider bears no responsibility for the correctness of the implemented code.
Screens requested beyond [NUMBER_OF_SCREENS] shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_SCREEN_FEE] per screen.

Where the Services include a design system, the Service Provider shall deliver a component library covering [DESIGN_SYSTEM_SCOPE]. The Client's license to reuse the design system components across additional products beyond [PROJECT_NAME] requires the Commercial Usage Clause.
Editable source files for the Deliverables shall be provided to the Client only where [SOURCE_FILES_INCLUDED] indicates inclusion, or upon payment of an additional fee of [SOURCE_FILES_FEE].

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.
The Client shall review each deliverable within [APPROVAL_PERIOD_DAYS] days. If the Client does not respond within this period, the deliverable shall be deemed approved.
The Client may request up to [INCLUDED_REVISION_ROUNDS] rounds of revisions at no additional charge. Additional rounds shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_REVISION_FEE] per round.
The Service Provider retains copyright in the Deliverables. Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives a [LICENSE_SCOPE] license to use the Deliverables for [PERMITTED_USE].
Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives a license to use the Deliverables, including any Design System components, for [PERMITTED_USE].

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — screen count explicit via Dynamic Field with Additional Screens pricing tied to exceeding it. Duplication: Design System vs Interactive Prototype kept distinct — the former addresses reusable components, the latter addresses interaction flow demonstration, genuinely separate deliverables. Loophole closed: Developer Support explicitly disclaims responsibility for implementation correctness, preventing designer liability for developer-caused bugs. Contradiction: none between Design System's reuse cross-reference and Commercial Usage — explicitly linked rather than left implicit. Missing protection: Design System scope explicitly tied back to Commercial Usage for reuse beyond the original project, closing a valuable-IP-leakage gap. Complexity: kept Additional Screens distinct from Extra Revision Rounds pattern used in Brand Identity, since screens are quantity-based scope, not iteration-based. Enforceability: developer support hours capped with explicit overage rate. Attack: Client reuses design system components across five other unrelated products without additional licensing — mitigated: Design System Clause explicitly requires the Commercial Usage Clause for reuse beyond the original project. Attack: Developer support results in a broken implementation and Client blames the UI designer — mitigated by explicit disclaimer of responsibility for implementation correctness. Attack: Client scope-creeps from 10 to 30 screens expecting no added cost — mitigated by Additional Screens per-screen pricing. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
